guards.shippedDocsEffectFreeGate: tests/unit/devops/docs-effect-residue.test.ts (package READMEs + root README + GETTING-STARTED carry no Effect install/pin/import/usage instruction — the ecosystem-zero claim now covers consumer docs, not just src)
guards.declaredDependencyClosureGate: tests/unit/devops/declared-dependency-closure.test.ts (every publishable package is dependency-closed across EVERY public runtime export, not just `.`)

verification.cleanRepositoryInstall: passed (rm -rf node_modules && pnpm install --frozen-lockfile; zero effect in node_modules or the .pnpm store; cold build passes)
verification.consumerSmoke: passed (packed @czap/core closure installed from ONLY its declared deps: Cell define/subscribe/dispose + S.struct/Codec.decode -> Result, executed from packed .d.ts + .js with zero effect installed and no undeclared extra dependency)
verification.reactivePublishAllocation: no measurable per-publish allocation under the allocation-gate harness with a live subscriber: 0.0000 B/op observed (CellKernel.fanout; was ~13 B/op via effect Queue.offerUnsafe — a finite-resolution benchmark, not a claim of mathematical zero)

notes.adr: ADR-0005 superseded by ADR-0042 (not erased) — the migration bridge from each Effect responsibility to its native owner.
notes.resolvedFinding: The packed-consumer proof surfaced a PRE-EXISTING, effect-unrelated packaging leak (issue #157, scar S8.1): @czap/core's canonical-cbor capsules eagerly imported 'fast-check' (via withArbitrary) while declaring it nowhere, so runtime execution INITIALLY required a manually-installed fast-check (declaredDependencyClosure: failed, runtimeExecution: passed_with_extra_dependency). RESOLVED: withArbitrary now takes fast-check as a thunk argument supplied by the harness — the capsules use only `import type` (erased); the MAIN entry runs on its declared deps alone. A permanent declared-dependency-closure gate was minted from the scar with the leak as its red fixture.
notes.resolvedFindingSubpathWidening: PR #158 (review finding #5) closed the REMAINING hole: the closure gate walked only exports['.'], so it never saw that @czap/core's PUBLIC ./harness subpath (arbitrary-from-schema) still imports fast-check at RUNTIME — a consumer importing @czap/core/harness still crashed while the gate stayed green. FULLY RESOLVED: the gate now walks EVERY public runtime export (skipping null/types-only/non-JS targets), which surfaces exactly two leaks — @czap/core/harness→fast-check and @czap/scene/dev→vite — both now declared as OPTIONAL peerDependencies (+ peerDependenciesMeta optional). The widened walk + the two optional-peer declarations make the ecosystem dependency-closed across all public subpaths, not just the main entry.
